summary: Miller’s pristine soundboard mix captures an uneven first set but a legendary second set highlighted by Dark Star, Attics of My Life and the Death Don’t > Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude sequence.
review: The Charlie Miller SBD restoration delivers crystal‑clear highs and a balanced mix that many reviewers claim beats even the official CD release. Musically the show is uneven – a solid but unremarkable first set – yet the second half erupts with a smoking Dark Star/DDHNM, an epic Attics of My Life encore and the otherworldly Death Don’t > Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude run that fans describe as “the stuff of Dead dreams.”

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, praising both the performance (especially the second set) and Miller’s mix; only a single low rating relates to download logistics, not content.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller's SBD mix is repeatedly called “better than the boxed set,” with high‑end detail and clarity; minor complaints concern lack of track numbers in the stream, not audio fidelity.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Dark Star
Death Don't > Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as solid but uneven, while the second set builds momentum with a powerful Playing in the Band jam that leads into an unforgettable Dark Star/Attics sequence and the transcendent Death Don't > Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude run.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his searing solos – especially the first solo in DDHNM and his work throughout the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s vocal performance is highlighted as outstanding; his keyboard contributions are praised indirectly via overall mix quality.
